rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex clauses only using identifiers explicitly visible in the current component scope.
Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes when describing interface behavior; use implementation annexes for assign clauses binding subcomponent outputs.
generic_rules: Contract clauses must reflect observable interface behavior, not internal implementation details.
Use exact case-sensitive names from the model index—never invent or copy identifiers from examples.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and must use valid keywords: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
Implementation-level bindings (assign) require the target to be a valid output port in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or paths in component type annexes—use only type-visible features.
